tasks.title: Analyze current IELTS Journey frontend for mobile and local-first readiness
tasks.description: Perform a detailed analysis of the current frontend-only IELTS Journey codebase to identify mobile usability gaps, local data handling, and architectural coupling to storage. Document current state of UI responsiveness, data storage patterns, and backend assumptions.
acceptanceCriteria: Documented list of mobile UI issues and missing mobile features
Inventory of current data storage methods and coupling
Identification of backend assumptions or fake backend logic
tasks.expectedResult: A comprehensive analysis report detailing current frontend limitations for mobile usage and local-first architecture gaps

outputPlan.description: Analysis report of current frontend mobile and local-first readiness

metadata.inputContext: Current frontend codebase and project documentation
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides baseline understanding of current frontend state for mobile and local-first improvements

tasks.title: Design mobile-first responsive UI and PWA architecture for IELTS Journey
tasks.description: Create a detailed design document specifying mobile-first responsive UI patterns, touch-friendly components, navigation structure, offline-first behavior, PWA support, dark mode, and error/loading states. Include recommendations for UI components and layout adjustments to support small screens without breaking layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Design document covers all core screens with mobile-first UI guidelines
Includes PWA support strategy and offline-first UX considerations
Defines UI states for loading, empty, and error conditions
tasks.expectedResult: A comprehensive mobile-first UI and PWA design document ready for implementation

outputPlan.description: Mobile-first responsive UI and PWA architecture design document

metadata.inputContext: Analysis report of current frontend mobile and local-first readiness
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides UI and PWA design blueprint for mobile-first refactoring

tasks.title: Design local-first data architecture with repository pattern and sync abstraction
tasks.description: Design a clean local-first data architecture for IELTS Journey using IndexedDB for structured data and localStorage for simple settings. Define repository interfaces for each data domain (profile, vocabulary, study plan, exercises, AI chat, settings) with clear separation from UI components. Include a sync layer abstraction to enable future backend integration without rewriting repositories.
acceptanceCriteria: Repository interfaces defined for all main data domains
Storage strategies specified (IndexedDB vs localStorage)
Sync abstraction layer design documented for future backend integration
tasks.expectedResult: A detailed local-first data architecture design document with repository and sync abstraction patterns

outputPlan.description: Local-first data architecture and repository pattern design document

metadata.inputContext: Analysis report of current frontend data storage and coupling
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides data layer design enabling local-first storage and future backend sync

tasks.title: Recommend best frontend-first mobile strategy for IELTS Journey
tasks.description: Produce a recommendation report comparing frontend-first mobile strategies: responsive web app, PWA, Capacitor wrapper, React Native/Expo, and Flutter. Evaluate each approach for fast delivery now, offline capability, local-first data support, and ease of future migration to backend and native apps. Recommend starting with mobile-first responsive web app plus PWA, then later Capacitor or React Native/Expo.
acceptanceCriteria: Comparison matrix of mobile strategies with pros and cons
Clear recommendation aligned with project goals
Explanation of migration path and trade-offs
tasks.expectedResult: A detailed recommendation report on the best frontend-first mobile strategy for IELTS Journey

metadata.inputContext: Analysis report of current frontend and mobile-first UI design
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Guides project direction for mobile-first frontend approach

tasks.title: Refactor core UI components for mobile-first responsive design
tasks.description: Refactor existing core UI components and layouts (dashboard, study plan, AI tutor, vocabulary notebook, exercises, settings) to implement mobile-first responsive design. Ensure touch-friendly buttons, simple navigation, no layout breaking on small screens, and support for dark mode. Add loading, empty, and error states as per design document.
acceptanceCriteria: Core screens render correctly on mobile viewport widths
UI elements are touch-friendly and accessible
Dark mode toggle works and persists
Loading, empty, and error states are implemented
tasks.expectedResult: Core UI components refactored for mobile-first responsive usage with PWA readiness

outputPlan.description: Refactored UI components for mobile-first responsiveness and PWA support
Refactored page layouts for mobile-first design

metadata.inputContext: Mobile-first UI and PWA design document
evidence: UI renders correctly on mobile viewport
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Makes the app usable and friendly on mobile devices with PWA support

tasks.title: Implement local-first repositories for user profile, study plan, vocabulary, and AI chat
tasks.description: Implement repository classes for user profile, target band, exam date, study plan, vocabulary notebook, saved articles, AI tutor chat history, and app settings. Use IndexedDB for structured data and localStorage for simple settings. Ensure stable IDs, separation from UI, and async APIs. Prepare repository interfaces to support future backend sync extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Repositories implemented with IndexedDB and localStorage as per design
Stable IDs used for all records
Repositories expose async CRUD methods
Unit tests cover repository logic
tasks.expectedResult: Local-first data repositories implemented and tested for core data domains

outputPlan.description: Local-first repositories for core data domains

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides clean local-first data access layer ready for future backend sync

tasks.title: Refactor UI components to use local-first repositories and decouple from storage
tasks.description: Refactor UI components and services to consume data exclusively via the new local-first repositories. Remove any direct IndexedDB or localStorage access from UI components. Ensure UI components remain agnostic of storage implementation to enable future backend integration without rewrite.
acceptanceCriteria: UI components consume data only via repository interfaces
No direct storage access in UI components
App behavior remains unchanged
Tests updated and passing
tasks.expectedResult: UI components fully decoupled from storage, using local-first repositories

outputPlan.description: UI components refactored to use repositories

evidence: No direct storage access in UI components
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Enables future backend integration by decoupling UI from storage

tasks.title: Add offline-first behavior and data persistence validation
tasks.description: Implement and validate offline-first behavior ensuring user can use all core features without internet. Verify data persistence across app reloads and browser restarts. Add tests or manual validation steps for offline usage scenarios including vocabulary saving, study progress updates, AI chat history, and settings persistence.
acceptanceCriteria: App fully functional offline for core features
Data persists after app reload and browser restart
Offline usage documented and tested
tasks.expectedResult: Offline-first behavior implemented and verified with persistent local data

outputPlan.description: Tests or validation scripts for offline-first behavior

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Ensures app works offline with reliable local data persistence

tasks.title: Add data export/import and backup features for local data
tasks.description: Implement UI and logic to allow users to export all local data (profile, vocabulary, study plan, AI chat, settings) as JSON file and import it back. Ensure data integrity and validation on import. Provide clear UI for backup and restore to support user data safety without backend.
acceptanceCriteria: User can export all local data to JSON file
User can import JSON backup and restore data correctly
UI provides clear instructions and error handling
tasks.expectedResult: Data export/import feature implemented and tested for local data backup

outputPlan.description: UI component for data export and import

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides user data safety and backup without backend

tasks.title: Prepare AI Tutor integration with local context and safe API key usage
tasks.description: Refactor AI Tutor feature to consume local user data context (target band, study plan, vocabulary, mistakes, progress) from repositories. Ensure AI API keys are not hardcoded or exposed in production. Support user-provided API keys with clear advanced usage warnings. Architect AI calls so they can be moved to backend safely later.
acceptanceCriteria: AI Tutor uses local repositories for context data
No sensitive API keys hardcoded in frontend
User can input personal AI API key with warning
AI call logic is modular and backend-migratable
tasks.expectedResult: AI Tutor integrated with local data context and safe API key handling

outputPlan.description: AI Tutor feature refactored for local context and safe API key usage
Component for user to input personal AI API key with warnings

metadata.inputContext: Local-first repositories and AI Tutor feature
evidence: AI Tutor works with local data
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Ensures AI Tutor works safely with local data and is backend-ready

tasks.title: Document what features can be done now without backend and what should wait
tasks.description: Create a clear documentation file explaining which IELTS Journey features are fully functional now with local-first architecture and which features require backend support and should be deferred. Include rationale and migration notes for future backend integration.
acceptanceCriteria: Documentation clearly lists features ready now and features deferred
Includes migration notes and backend dependency explanations
tasks.expectedResult: Documentation file clarifying current and future feature readiness with backend dependency

outputPlan.description: Documentation of features doable now and features waiting for backend

metadata.inputContext: Local-first data architecture and mobile strategy recommendations
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides clarity on current app capabilities and future backend plans

tasks.title: Create future backend sync design including sync queue, conflict resolution, and stable IDs
tasks.description: Design a future backend sync architecture including a sync queue for local changes, conflict resolution strategies, stable ID generation for all records, and separation of local data logic from UI. Document API contract considerations and how to extend repositories to support backend sync without breaking UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Sync queue design documented with change tracking
Conflict resolution strategy described
Stable ID generation approach specified
API contract extension guidelines included
tasks.expectedResult: Comprehensive future backend sync design document ready for implementation

outputPlan.description: Future backend sync architecture design document

metadata.finalOutputContribution: Prepares the app for smooth future backend sync integration

tasks.title: Review and finalize the mobile-first local-first architecture plan
tasks.description: Conduct a thorough review of all design and implementation plans for mobile-first responsive UI, local-first data architecture, AI Tutor integration, offline-first behavior, and future backend sync readiness. Ensure completeness, consistency, and alignment with project goals. Prepare a final summary report.
acceptanceCriteria: All design documents are consistent and complete
Summary report highlights key decisions and next steps
No conflicting recommendations
tasks.expectedResult: Finalized architecture plan and summary report ready for implementation

outputPlan.description: Final architecture plan and summary report

metadata.inputContext: All previous design and analysis documents
metadata.finalOutputContribution: Provides a clear, agreed-upon plan for mobile-first local-first IELTS Journey
